mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, KIT, PDGFR and other kinases to block AML cell proliferation and survival
additional_notes: Approved for FLT3-mutated AML; strong activity in MOLM-13

mechanism: Selectively inhibits FLT3 and AXL signaling to reduce proliferation of FLT3-mut AML cells
additional_notes: Approved for relapsed/refractory FLT3-mutated AML; potent in MOLM-13

mechanism: Highly selective inhibition of FLT3 kinase, particularly FLT3-ITD, leading to AML cell death
additional_notes: Also known as AC220; very potent against FLT3-ITD AML cell lines including MOLM-13

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, RAF, VEGFR, PDGFR and other kinases, disrupting proliferation and survival signaling
additional_notes: Used off-label in FLT3-mutated AML; published MOLM-13 data; 10 nM–2 µM expected active range

mechanism: Targets FLT3, KIT, VEGFR, PDGFR and related kinases to inhibit AML cell growth
additional_notes: Reported FLT3 inhibition and antiproliferative effects in AML cell lines

mechanism: Inhibits SRC and ABL family kinases and other signaling kinases involved in AML cell survival
additional_notes: Shows activity in some FLT3-mut and other AML cell lines

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2 to block JAK-STAT signaling implicated in subsets of AML
additional_notes: Shows antiproliferative effects in some AML models; expected active range ~0.1–5 µM

mechanism: Selectively inhibits anti-apoptotic BCL-2, promoting apoptosis of AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as ABT-199; approved for AML in combination; robust single-agent activity in many AML lines including MOLM-13; expected active range ~1–300 nM

mechanism: Inhibits exportin 1 (XPO1/CRM1), leading to nuclear retention of tumor suppressor proteins and induction of apoptosis
additional_notes: Nuclear export inhibition; preclinical and clinical AML data; expected active range ~20 nM–1 µM

mechanism: Inhibits multiple histone deacetylases, causing epigenetic modulation and apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Strong epigenetic effects; cytotoxic to AML blasts at low nM; expected active range ~5–200 nM

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to alter chromatin structure and induce apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA; epigenetic modulation in AML cell lines; expected active range ~0.5–10 µM

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and inhibits DNA methyltransferase, causing hypomethylation and cytotoxicity after DNA incorporation
additional_notes: Standard AML drug; expected active range in vitro (72–96 h) ~50 nM–5 µM

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and RNA, inhibiting DNA methyltransferase and altering RNA function to induce anti-proliferative effects
additional_notes: Standard of care in AML/MDS; expected active range (72–96 h) ~0.5–20 µM

mechanism: Primarily inhibits Bruton's tyrosine kinase, with additional off-target kinase effects that can disrupt AML signaling
additional_notes: Reduces viability in subsets of AML cell lines; expected active range ~0.5–10 µM

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK3, modulating JAK-STAT signaling involved in myeloid malignancies
additional_notes: Repurposing interest in myeloid malignancies; expected active range ~1–20 µM

mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL and KIT, as well as other kinases implicated in AML cell survival
additional_notes: Reported activity in various AML cell lines; expected active range ~50 nM–3 µM

mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL, KIT, and PDGFR signaling pathways, reducing proliferation in susceptible AML subgroups
additional_notes: Applicable to KIT- or PDGFR-dependent AML; modest direct MOLM-13 activity possible; expected active range ~0.5–10 µM

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 to block MAPK/ERK signaling, leading to antiproliferative effects in RAS/MAPK-driven AML models
additional_notes: Expected active range ~1–200 nM; MAPK pathway active in many AMLs

mechanism: Inhibits mTORC1, reducing PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway signaling required for AML cell growth and survival
additional_notes: mTORC1 inhibitor; expected active range ~1–500 nM; shows AML cytotoxicity in vitro

category: AMPK activator / mitochondrial complex I inhibitor
mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial complex I and activates AMPK, leading to energetic stress and anti-proliferative effects in AML cells at high concentrations
additional_notes: Repurposing candidate; expected active range in vitro ~0.5–10 mM in various cancer cell lines including myeloid
